data.title: Robo.ai and Changer.ae launch Roboy339, first smart vehicle with digital wallet, revealed at TOKEN2049. #innovation
data.description: In Dubai, United Arab Emirates, on October 3, 2025, Robo.ai Inc. (Nasdaq: AIIO) and a Digital Asset Custodian regulated by the UAE, Changer.ae, introduced "Roboy339" at TOKEN2049. This groundbreaking smart vehicle is the first of its kind to feature a personal digital wallet. The collaboration between Robo.ai and Changer.ae commenced in August 2025.

data.title: Scania introduces high-floor battery-electric platform at Busworld for first zero-emission coaches. Major step towards electrification.
data.description: In SÖDERTÄLJE, Sweden, on October 3, 2025, Scania made a significant move towards electrifying new market areas by revealing its latest high-floor battery-electric bus and coach platform at Busworld Europe. This unveiling marks a major milestone for Scania in diversifying its offerings with electric vehicles. The development of the new BEV platform is a crucial step in Scania's commitment to sustainability and reducing carbon emissions. This move highlights Scania's dedication to innovation and staying at the forefront of the evolving automotive industry.

data.title: Q3 saw a 37% decrease in crypto hack losses to $509 million. Million-dollar incidents spiked in September, mostly from exchange and DeFi hacks.
data.description: In the third quarter, losses due to crypto hacks decreased by 37% to $509 million. However, September witnessed a significant spike in million-dollar incidents, primarily driven by hacks targeting exchanges and DeFi platforms.

data.title: Stock futures rise on 3rd day of shutdown, led by Nasdaq, Dow. Market shrugs off concerns as AI boosts optimism. Nasdaq 100 futures up 0.26%, Dow up 0.24%. Trump proposes rebate checks funded by tariff revenue. Treasury bond yields - 10-year: 4.09%, 2-year: 3.55%. Fed likely to cut rates in October meeting - 97.8% probability. S&P 500 hits record high, with Nvidia and AMD stocks surging. Dow up 0.17% at 46,519.72, S&P 500 up 0.062% at 6,715.35, Nasdaq up 0.39% at 22,844.05, Russell 2000 up 0.66%.
data.description: U.S. stock futures rose after record advances on Thursday. AI-led optimism drove markets higher despite most S&P 500 sectors declining, with tech and communication services advancing. President Trump proposed issuing rebate checks to Americans funded by tariff revenue to pay down national debt. 10-year Treasury bond yield was at 4.09%, with a 97.8% likelihood of a Fed rate cut in October. Futures changes: Dow Jones +0.24%, S&P 500 +0.25%, Nasdaq 100 +0.26%, Russell 2000 +0.42%. S&P 500 ETF Trust and QQQ Trust ETF rose in premarket. Nvidia and AMD stocks surged, Intel may add AMD as a customer. Dow ended higher at 46,519.72, S&P 500 at 6,715.35, Nasdaq at 22,844.05, and Russell 2000 at 2,458.49.

data.title: AKVA group ASA starting buyback of up to 60,000 shares for employee share programme announced on Sept 23, 2025.
data.description: On September 23, 2025, AKVA group ASA (referred to as "AKVA group" or the "Company") disclosed their commencement of repurchasing a maximum of 60,000 shares belonging to the company. This initiative aims to utilize the shares within its employee share program.

data.title: Legislators in NY aim to impose energy tax on Bitcoin mining, potentially prompting miners to relocate due to high electricity costs.
data.description: Proposed legislation in New York is aiming to crack down on cryptocurrency miners due to their significant electricity consumption. The new laws could result in a large number of miners moving away from the state to avoid the strict regulations. This move comes as a response to the miners' high energy usage, which has been a growing concern. The potential exodus of miners could have a noticeable impact on the industry in the state, with estimates suggesting that it could affect hundreds of mining operations. The proposed laws are part of a broader effort to address the environmental and economic implications of cryptocurrency mining.
